extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a becomes true only via r4, which requires the conjunction polypharmacy AND ckd_dialysis AND seizure_history AND low_ejection_fraction. But r2 (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true whenever polypharmacy is true. Since polypharmacy is a necessary conjunct for r4 firing, every input that makes rec_drug_a true also triggers r2, making rec_drug_b true. There are no other rules or higher-priority overrides for these outputs (each is set by a single rule; defaults only apply when unset). Thus the conditions for rec_drug_a are a strict subset of the conditions for rec_drug_b, so they cannot be mutually exclusive. Counterexample: polypharmacy=true, ckd_dialysis=true, seizure_history=true, low_ejection_fraction=true (prior_gi_bleed and qt_prolongation are irrelevant, set false). r4 fires -> rec_drug_a=true; r2 fires -> rec_drug_b=true; both true violates mutual exclusion.
